chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS C.P No.S-253 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A No.9118/2024. 03.03.2026 Mr. Sahib Khan Panhwar, advocate for attorney of petitioners a/w attorney Muhammad Rafique. Ms. Ambreen Siyal, Advocate for private respondents a/w respondent No.17. Mr. Muhammad Shareef Solangi, Assistant A.G Sindh. = Heard. By consent Restoration Application (M.A No.9118/2024) is allowed and the matter is restored at its original position as it was on 10.12.2024. After arguing the matter at length, both the learned counsel, under the instructions of their respective clients, state that they would be satisfied if the matter is remanded back to the trial court for decision on merits strictly in accordance with law and to decide the case within the stipulated period, subject to payment of cost of Rs.100,000/-. In view of the above and by consent, the instant petition is allowed and the impugned Order dated 24.02.2024 passed by the learned Additional District Judge, Shahdadpur in Civil Revision Application No.08/2022 and Order dated 29.01.2022 passed by the learned Senior Civil Judge, Shahdadpur in F.C Suit No.206/2018 are set-aside, and the plaint is restored to its original position and the matter is remanded back to the learned trial court, with direction to conclude the trial within a period of 2 90 days from the date of receipt of this Order, whereby no unnecessary adjournment shall be granted to any of the parties, however, subject to cost of Rs.100,000/- to be paid by the petitioners to the respondents. The petitioners shall deposit Rs.50,000/- on 16.03.2026 and the remaining Rs.50,000/- be deposited on or before 06.04.2026 with the Additional Registrar of this Court under proper receipt and to be disbursed to the respondents after due verification.

In view of the above and by consent, the instant petition is allowed and the impugned Order dated 24.02.2024 passed by the learned Additional District Judge, Shahdadpur in Civil Revision Application No.08/2022 and Order dated 29.01.2022 passed by the learned Senior Civil Judge, Shahdadpur in F.C Suit No.206/2018 are set-aside, and the plaint is restored to its original position and the matter is remanded back to the learned trial court, with direction to conclude the trial within a period of 2 90 days from the date of receipt of this Order, whereby no unnecessary adjournment shall be granted to any of the parties, however, subject to cost of Rs.100,000/- to be paid by the petitioners to the respondents. The petitioners shall deposit Rs.50,000/- on 16.03.2026 and the remaining Rs.50,000/- be deposited on or before 06.04.2026 with the Additional Registrar of this Court under proper receipt and to be disbursed to the respondents after due verification. It is specifically recorded that in case of non-payment of the above consented costs by the petitioners, the matter shall be deemed to have been dismissed for non-compliance of the said Order. The instant petition is disposed of in above terms. JUDGE *Faisal*
